summary: Pattern: robil by som (I would do), robila by si (you would do, F), robili by sme (we would do)
3rd person has no auxiliary: robil by (he would do), robila by (she would), robili by (they would)
by + som/si/sme/ste — same auxiliaries as past tense, just with 'by' inserted before them
Reflexive sa/si goes BETWEEN participle and by: učil sa by som (I would learn)
Negation: ne- on the participle — nerobil by som (I wouldn't do)
Aspect works same as past: imperfective = hypothetical process, perfective = hypothetical result
písal by som (I would be writing) vs napísal by som (I would write/finish)
byť conditional: bol by som (I would be), bola by (she would be)
mohol by som = I could (very common polite form)
Irregular participles are same as past tense: išiel, jedol, pil, mohol, vedel
Word order flexible: robil by som to = to by som robil (emphasis shifts)

rules.title: How the Conditional Works
rules.text: The conditional uses the SAME l-participle as past tense, but inserts 'by' before the personal auxiliary. If you know the past tense, you already know 90% of the conditional.
examples: Past: Robil som to – I did it
Conditional: Robil by som to – I would do it
Past: Prišla – She came
Conditional: Prišla by – She would come

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs
rules.text: Reflexive sa/si goes between the participle and 'by'. Pattern: participle + sa/si + by + auxiliary.
examples: Učil sa by som slovenčinu – I would learn Slovak
Umyla sa by si ráno – You would wash in the morning (F)
Obliekli sa by sme rýchlo – We would get dressed quickly

rules.title: Negation
rules.text: Add ne- to the participle. Everything else stays the same.
examples: Nerobil by som to – I wouldn't do it
Neprišla by – She wouldn't come
Neučil sa by som – I wouldn't learn

rules.title: Aspect in Conditional
rules.text: Same logic as past tense: imperfective for hypothetical ongoing/repeated actions, perfective for hypothetical completed actions.

rows: písal by som – I would be writing
napísal by som – I would write (finish)
čítal by som – I would be reading
prečítal by som – I would read (finish)
jedol by som – I would be eating
zjedol by som – I would eat it all
robil by som – I would be doing
urobil by som – I would do (complete)
učil sa by som – I would be studying
naučil sa by som – I would learn (master)

examples: Čítal by som knihu celý deň – I would be reading a book all day (process)
Prečítal by som knihu – I would read the book (finish it)

rules.title: Conditional of byť (to be)
rules.text: Uses the irregular participle bol/bola/bolo/boli — same as past tense.
examples: Bol by som šťastný – I would be happy
Bola by som unavená – I would be tired (F)
Boli by sme doma – We would be at home

rules.title: Irregular Verbs in Conditional
rules.text: Same irregular participles as past tense. If you know the past tense form, just add 'by' + auxiliary.
examples: Išiel by som domov – I would go home
Mohol by som pomôcť – I could help
Vedel by som odpoveď – I would know the answer
Mohla by si mi pomôcť? – Could you help me? (F, very common polite form)

rules.title: Word Order Flexibility
rules.text: Standard order is participle + by + auxiliary, but 'by + auxiliary' can move to second position for questions or emphasis. The meaning stays the same.
examples: Robil by som to – I would do it (neutral)
To by som robil – That's what I would do (emphasis on 'that')
Kedy by si prišiel? – When would you come?
Mohol by si mi pomôcť? – Could you help me? (polite request)
